Title: Mitochondrial formate dehydrogenase links mitochondrial and cytosolic one carbon metabolism via a one-carbon shunt

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: UBQ10 promoter (constitutive) for FDH1-mCherry overexpression; native FDH1 promoter (~1–1.265 kb upstream) for GUS reporter
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: FDH1 is a promising engineering target to improve growth, photosynthetic capacity, and tolerance to exogenous formate by increasing mitochondrial formate oxidation capacity and balancing one-carbon flux between mitochondria and cytosol. Overexpression accelerated growth and increased biomass, suggesting value for metabolic engineering of one-carbon metabolism and photorespiration-related performance. Likely best deployed with companion engineering of cytosolic formate-assimilating steps such as FTHFL/MTHFD1 and photorespiratory genes; monitor chlorophyll composition, respiration, and root traits because both loss and overexpression affected root length and one-carbon/redox balance.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FDH1 maintains homeostatic one-carbon metabolism by regulating a mitochondrial-cytosolic formate shunt; loss of FDH1 causes growth defects, reduced photosynthetic performance, altered chlorophyll a/b ratio, and hypersensitivity to exogenous formate, whereas overexpression enhances growth and photosynthetic parameters.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout mutants; constitutive overexpression; recombinant protein enzyme assays; mitochondrial activity assays; subcellular localization by GFP/mCherry; promoter-GUS expression analysis; physiological phenotyping; gas exchange; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ambient CO2 growth; low CO2 (100 ppm) photorespiratory conditions for metabolomics; short-day conditions; exogenous sodium formate treatment (5 mM); illuminated leaf versus heterotrophic/night context discussed for one-carbon flux interpretation
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: No direct epigenetic regulation of FDH1 demonstrated. Paper discusses that altered cytosolic one-carbon metabolism in mthfd1 can cause DNA hypomethylation, implying downstream methylation consequences of one-carbon imbalance. FDH1 itself was noted from prior studies as lysine-acetylated, but this is post-translational, not epigenetic.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Primarily expressed in cotyledons, apex of true leaf vascular bundles, and root tissues, especially the differentiation zone; transcript abundance behaves acyclic to GLDT during the light period.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily expressed in cotyledons, apex of true leaf vascular bundles, and root tissues, especially the differentiation zone.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: None experimentally validated in this paper; functionally linked to GLDT; discussed in context of FTHFL, SHM1, SHM2, SHM3, GDC
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Mitochondrial proteomics dataset referenced for N-terminal processing (Kuhnert et al. 2020); GC-EI-QTOF-MS metabolomics in this study; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FDH1 is encoded by a single Arabidopsis gene. The study rejects previous claims of dual chloroplast/mitochondrial targeting and supports exclusive mitochondrial localization. Despite clear growth and physiological phenotypes, steady-state pools of glycine, serine, methionine, folates, formate, and SAM were unchanged, supporting a flux-control rather than pool-size-control role. FDH1 showed no allosteric regulation by tested photorespiratory metabolites and no effect of oxidizing/reducing conditions in vitro. A lysine acetylation site was discussed but K184R mimic did not alter activity.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout causes reduced growth, lower photosynthesis, lower dark respiration, reduced chlorophyll a/b ratio, and formate hypersensitivity. Overexpression improved biomass but also reduced primary root length, indicating possible developmental tradeoffs and flux/redox redistribution.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FDH1 encodes a mitochondrial NAD-dependent formate dehydrogenase with formate as preferred substrate and a pH optimum of 7.5. 2. FDH1 is required to prevent formate toxicity and to sustain normal growth, photosynthesis, respiration, and chlorophyll balance. 3. Genetic and physiological evidence supports FDH1 as a regulator of a mitochondrial-cytosolic one-carbon shunt mediated by formate exchange rather than a determinant of steady-state one-carbon metabolite pools.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Formate-derived one-carbon units (C1-folate pool/SAM-linked one-carbon metabolism)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Distributed across mitochondria and cytosol; downstream one-carbon products function cell-wide
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports biosynthesis of nucleotides, amino acids, cofactors, methylation reactions, chlorophyll-related methyl transfer, and cellular redox-linked metabolic integration

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: UBQ10 promoter (constitutive) for FDH1-mCherry overexpression; native FDH1 promoter for GUS reporter
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: One-carbon metabolism / folate-mediated C1 metabolism / mitochondrial formate oxidation branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Increase FDH1 activity to enhance mitochondrial formate detoxification, strengthen one-carbon flux homeostasis, and improve growth/photosynthetic performance. Best combined with engineering of complementary one-carbon assimilation and photorespiratory steps to avoid bottlenecks in formate reassimilation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes formate to CO2 using NAD+ as electron acceptor, producing NADH; glyoxylate is a weak secondary substrate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls mitochondrial formate oxidation and thereby influences one-carbon flux between mitochondria and cytosol, plant growth, photosynthesis, chlorophyll composition, and formate tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purified recombinant enzyme kinetics and substrate specificity; mitochondrial activity assays; CRISPR knockout and overexpression phenotyping; localization assays
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies/removes one-carbon intermediate pool by regulating formate availability and oxidation; controls flux toward cytosolic and cellular folate-dependent end uses rather than directly synthesizing a terminal nutrient product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ambient CO2; low CO2 photorespiratory condition; exogenous 5 mM sodium formate; light/dark and tissue context considered in metabolic model
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: No epigenetic mechanism shown. Post-translational lysine acetylation reported previously; K184R mimic had no activity change in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in cotyledons, leaf vascular bundle apex, and root differentiation zone
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in cotyledons, leaf vascular bundle apex, and root differentiation zone
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: No direct interactors validated; functionally connected to GLDT; proposed network links with FTHFL, SHM1, SHM2, SHM3
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Side-route / shunt control step in mitochondrial one-carbon metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GC-EI-QTOF-MS metabolomics; referenced mitochondrial proteomics dataset (no accession provided)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: High Km for formate (~3.85–4.11 mM) suggests low-affinity detoxification/flux-balancing role or localized high formate microenvironments. No activity with NADP. No detectable allosteric regulation by tested photorespiratory metabolites or redox reagents.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Mitochondrial formate oxidation branch of the C1 shunt linking mitochondrial THF cycle output to cytosolic formate assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excessive or insufficient activity may perturb root development and alter respiratory/photosynthetic balance; overexpression did not change steady-state metabolite pools but may redirect redox and carbon flux.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control/regulatory step for the C1 shunt, but not definitively established as the sole rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FDH1 is a bona fide NAD-dependent mitochondrial formate dehydrogenase. 2. Knockout abolishes mitochondrial FDH activity and causes formate hypersensitivity and reduced growth/photosynthesis, while overexpression has opposite effects. 3. FDH1 likely regulates mitochondrial-cytosolic formate exchange within a one-carbon shunt rather than changing bulk steady-state metabolite levels.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Feeds nucleotide, amino acid, cofactor, SAM, and methylation metabolism while coupling redox state to one-carbon flux

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GLDT is a relevant companion target for engineering photorespiratory one-carbon flux together with FDH1, because it supplies mitochondrial 5,10-methylene-THF upstream of formate generation. However, this paper provides only indirect network evidence, so deployment should rely on external functional data.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GLDT is discussed as an upstream provider of photorespiratory one-carbon units that feed the mitochondrial THF cycle and proposed formate shunt, but no direct phenotype was tested here.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript comparison during light phase; literature-supported pathway context only
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent upstream source of mitochondrial C1 units through photorespiration; important for flux into the proposed formate shunt but not directly demonstrated as the decisive regulator in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript abundance discussed over the light period; FDH1 behaved acyclic to GLDT
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not specifically reported in this paper beyond light-phase transcript behavior
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Glycine decarboxylase complex components; functionally linked to FDH1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Photorespiratory generation of mitochondrial one-carbon units
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcript comparison mentioned; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GLDT encodes the GDC T-protein required for production of 5,10-methylene-THF in mitochondria; included because the paper explicitly links its activity to mitochondrial formate generation in the proposed C1 shunt.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: None directly established; acts enzymatically within GDC rather than as a classic regulator
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Indirect metabolic control via provision of upstream C1 intermediates
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Metabolic flux-associated upstream controller / complex component
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Not a gene regulator; increases mitochondrial production of 5,10-methylene-THF upstream of formate production
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GLDT is discussed as the GDC T-protein generating 5,10-methylene-THF in photorespiration. 2. Its light-phase transcript pattern differs from FDH1, consistent with FDH1 acting as a downstream flux regulator rather than a coexpressed structural partner. 3. The paper uses GLDT to support the model that photorespiration supplies mitochondrial C1 units for the formate shunt.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mitochondrial one-carbon metabolism feeding cytosolic C1 use
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports amino acid, nucleotide, methylation, and redox-linked metabolism
